text: Code reviews are an essential practice for any software development team. During a code review, one or more developers check a teammate's code before it is added to the main project. The most obvious benefit is catching bugs early. It is much easier and cheaper to fix a mistake found during a review than after the code has been released. However, the benefits go beyond finding errors. Code reviews are a powerful tool for knowledge sharing. Junior developers learn from more experienced team members by seeing how problems are solved differently. Finally, reviews help raise the overall quality of the team's code, as everyone follows the same standards and suggests improvements.
